printer_verbatim: Islamia Press, Lahore, and Central Printing Works, Lahore.
printer: Islamia Press and Central Printing Works
author: Rahmat Ali, Rahmat, Maulvi
title: Wafa-i-Rahmat
gloss: A voluminous book in verse on the Jubilee of Her late Majesty Queen Victoria together with versified translation of the Proclamation of 1858 and of those subsequently issued.
copyright: M. Rahmat Ali, Rahmat, Dera Ghazi Khan; No. 174, dated the 21st November, 1910.
notes: 598 pages of versified imperial constitutionalism — the 1858 Proclamation itself rendered into Urdu verse and copyrighted; loyalist monumentalism from Dera Ghazi Khan, split across two presses.
marks: X; pencil '68' right margin (top of page)

flags.issue: copyright cell partially cut; date line appears to print '1911' where 1910 is expected — transcribed as 1910

author: Riaz-ud-din Ahmad, Munshi
title: Mazaq-i-Sukhan
publisher: L. Diwan Chand, Proprietor, Kutabkhana T. C. Gujrati
notes: Same author as the dramas at p19 s17–18; same Kutabkhana T. C. Gujrati publisher as s64.

author: Suraj Narayan, Mehr, Munshi
title: Kalam-i-Mehr, Jild-i-Sani
gloss: A collection of poems on subjects chiefly connected with Vedantism and Sufiism.
publisher: The author, who is Reporter on Books, Education Department, Punjab
copyright: L. Suraj Narayan, Mehr, Reporter on Books, Education Department, Punjab, Lahore; No. 16—, dated the — November, 1910.
notes: THE REPORTER ON BOOKS HIMSELF: the official who compiles and signs these catalogs registers his own Vedanta-and-Sufism verse collection at 8,000 copies (largest Urdu poetry run of the quarter), copyrights it, and catalogs it — the observation operator entering its own record. Entry prints date '4th November, 1911' (misprint for 1910). Bracket note: previous part noted in entry No. 21 at page 39 of the Catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th June, 1909.

flags.issue: printed '4th November, 1911' — impossible for a Dec 1910 quarter; read as 1910

title: Bahar Punjab, Hissa Pehla
gloss: Select songs of the various dramas of the Indian theatres, Part I.
notes: Parsi-theatre song repertoire transliterated into Gurmukhi — commercial stage music crossing the script divide (music-circulation data point); serial 71 continues Urdu—Poetry across the script subsection.

title: Bahar Punjab, Part II
notes: Dash-continuation of s71; consecutive regs 1188–1190 with s74 — one registration batch by Jot Singh Sant Singh.

author: Chandu Ram, Malik
title: Siri Ram Bilas
gloss: Devotional and admonitory verses adapted to Indian music.
copyright: Mahant Fateh Chand, Montgomery; No. 162, dated the 25th October, 1910.
notes: Catalogued as 'adapted to Indian music' — the registrar's own category for singable devotional text; mahant-copyrighted from Montgomery.

title: Majmua Bahar Sukhan
gloss: A collection of Qazals or love poems.
notes: Urdu ghazals in Gurmukhi — the ghazal readership beyond the Persian script.

title: Jadid Turki Daur
gloss: A translation by M. Muhammad Insha-Ullah, Editor of the 'Watan,' Lahore, of Knight's The 'Awakening of Turkey.'
copyright: M. Muhammad Insha-Ullah, Lahore; No. 170, dated the 25th October, 1910.
notes: E. F. Knight's The Awakening of Turkey (on the 1908 Young Turk revolution) translated by the editor of the pan-Islamist weekly Watan, 536 pp., copyrighted — international Muslim political consciousness routed through an English war correspondent's book; only POLITICS entry so far this quarter.
